description_long: This obelisk is a tall, monolithic structure with a square cross-section, rising from a broad, rectangular base. The base is composed of a thick, stepped plinth with a slightly protruding lower edge, giving it a solid and monumental appearance. The shaft of the obelisk is vertically faceted, with clean, sharp edges and a uniform stone texture throughout. On two opposing sides of the base, there are short, rectangular extensions, possibly for structural or decorative purposes. The surface is a consistent light gray, resembling polished or finely cut stone, and there are no visible inscriptions or decorative carvings. The overall design is minimalist and geometric, emphasizing solidity and permanence.
description: A tall, square-sectioned stone obelisk with a broad, stepped rectangular base and minimalist design. The surface is smooth, light gray, and unadorned, with two short side extensions at the base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main shaft rising from the center of a broad, stepped base with a prominent rectangular extension at the bottom center.
description_view_1: Side view revealing a short, rectangular extension on the left side of the base and the clean, faceted shaft above.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the symmetrical base and shaft, with no visible extensions or features on this side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a rectangular extension on the right side of the base, mirroring the feature seen in view 1.
attribute_base_design: The base is a broad, rectangular plinth with a stepped profile and a slightly protruding lower edge. It features two short, rectangular extensions on opposite sides, adding to its geometric solidity. The material appears to be the same light gray stone or concrete as the shaft.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is flat and square, matching the shaft's cross-section, with no decorative features or tapering.
